q: The octa rule does not apply to ________ elements.

A coordinate bond, or dative bond, is a special type of ________ bond.
A metal loses electrons to form ________.
The angle between two bonds in a linear molecule is ________.

In ionic compounds, the cations and anions are held together by strong ________.

According to ________, no more two electrons in an atom can have same orbital.

London dispersion force is also called ________ interaction.
A triple bond is formed by sharing ________ pairs of electrons.
The direction of the dipole can also be shown by the sign ________.

In covalent bonding, the ________ valence electrons are not involved in bonding.

In some substances in solids, atoms are joined together by single covalent bonds, exciting ________ structures.
Covalent bonds are formed when electrons are ________.
The molecular shape of BeCl₂ is ________.
The attractive forces that hold atoms together in compounds are also known as ________.
The length of the bond is determined by the number of ________ electrons.
The shape of the molecule of NH₃ is ________ by VSEPR model.
The hydrogen bond is ________ than a covalent bond.
Each carbon bonded layer is formed with ________ rings of carbon atoms, in graphite.
The non-metal atom readily gains electrons to form ________.
A crystal lattice that shows the ________ pattern of the entire crystal.
The shape of water is ________.
The crystalline structure of an ionic solid is called a ________.
________ is a pair of equal in magnitude and opposite charges separated by a distance.
A graphite has a layer and ________ structure.
The greater the charge on the ions, the ________ electrostatic attraction.
The higher the number of bonded electrons, the ________ the bond length.
The VSEPR theory is used to predict the ________ of covalent molecules.

Fullerene (C₆₀), carbon nanotubes, and graphene do not exist as ________ structures.
The degree of bond polarity is measured as a ________ of a bond.
A covalent bond formed is non-polar if the two atoms have ________ electronegativity.
The bond length of the single bond is the ________.
In the CO₂ molecule, there are ________ bonding pairs without lone pairs.
________ unit cell is a cubic cell with an atom in each corner and another in the centre of the cube.
The hydrogen bond is the ________ of all intermolecular forces.
When atoms share an unequal number of electrons between two atoms, a ________ covalent bond is formed.
The greater the charge on the ions, the ________ ionic bond.
________ is a measure of the ability of an atom to attract the shared electrons to itself.
In expanded valence shell molecule has valence electrons of more than ________.
________ unit cell is a cubic cell with an atom in each corner of the cube.
The strength of metallic bond is ________ than that of an ionic bond.
BF₃ has a ________ shape.

The higher the number of bonded electrons, the ________ the attraction between the two atoms.

Dipole-Dipole forces are ________ than London dispersion forces.

The amount of energy required to ________ an electron from a gaseous atom.

________ is the smallest repeating unit of a crystal lattice.

________ are weak intermolecular forces that bind molecules together.
